description: Static architecture strata for kast. Project dependencies must point to the same or a lower layer; low layers must keep production external dependencies allow-listed.

rules.lowLayerExternalPolicy: L0 and L1 production external dependencies must be allow-listed with rationale. L2 and above may use external libraries, but core logic must stay in lower layers.

L0.name: Pure Types
L0.purpose: Kotlin interfaces, ADTs, value classes, enums, type aliases, validation results, derivation states, error types, and pure functions over those types.

L1.name: Internal Libraries
L1.purpose: Owned side-effect-free computation, data transformation, and domain logic that depends only on L0.

L2.name: External Libraries
L2.purpose: Quarantined adapters around essential third-party libraries so callers do not couple to replaceable implementation details.

L3.name: Host-Specific Code
L3.purpose: Host runtime concerns such as filesystem access, workspace discovery, background work, lifecycle, and PSI/K2 helpers.

L4.name: Integration and Adapters
L4.purpose: CLI, JSON-RPC, IntelliJ plugin, Gradle/build, and other adapters that translate between external systems and lower-layer semantics.

L5.name: Tests and Fixtures
L5.purpose: Test-only fixtures, contract assertions, parity tests, and utilities that verify observable behavior.

L6.name: Documentation and Site
L6.purpose: Source documentation, architecture notes, published guidance, and generated site output.

:analysis-api.rationale: Shared contract models and typed request/response surfaces are the current pure-type seam. Coroutines and Hoplite are allow-listed because existing client/config helpers still live beside contract types until they can be split behind narrower seams.

:index-store.rationale: SQLite persistence is an owned quarantine around an external storage library.

:backend-shared.rationale: Shared host-runtime analysis helpers compile against PSI/K2 APIs supplied by the runtime host.

:backend-standalone.rationale: The standalone backend currently combines host lifecycle with server integration and daemon adapter concerns.

:analysis-server.rationale: JSON-RPC transport and descriptor dispatch adapt protocol requests to lower-layer semantics.

:backend-intellij.rationale: The IntelliJ plugin adapts IDE lifecycle, project services, and plugin packaging to the shared contract.

:kast-cli.rationale: The CLI is the operator-facing adapter for command parsing, lifecycle orchestration, and distribution.

:shared-testing.rationale: Fake backend fixtures and shared contract assertions are test-only surfaces.

:parity-tests.rationale: Parity tests verify behavior across implementations without production ownership.

pathLayers.rationale: Copilot extensions and hooks adapt repository rules into the agent runtime.
Documentation source and generated site output live outside production code.
Tests and fixtures may depend on all production layers.
